title: सूर्य गायत्री
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूर्य गायत्री ॥

ॐ भास्कराय विद्महे
महातेजाय धीमहि।
तन्नो सूर्यः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ द्वितीय सूर्य गायत्री ॥

ॐ आदित्याय विद्महे
दिवाकराय धीमहि।
तन्नो सूर्यः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ तृतीय सूर्य गायत्री ॥

ॐ सप्ताश्वाय विद्महे
सहस्रकिरणाय धीमहि।
तन्नो रविः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ इति श्री सूर्य गायत्री सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Surya Gayatri ||

Om Bhaskaraya Vidmahe
Maha-tejaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Suryah Prachodayat.

|| Dvitiya Surya Gayatri ||

Om Adityaya Vidmahe
Divakaraya Dhimahi.
Tanno Suryah Prachodayat.

|| Tritiya Surya Gayatri ||

Om Sapta-ashvaya Vidmahe
Sahasra-kiranaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Ravih Prachodayat.

|| Iti Shri Surya Gayatri Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: हम भास्कर (सूर्य) को जानते हैं, महातेजस्वी का ध्यान करते हैं, वह सूर्य हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करें। हम आदित्य को जानते हैं, दिवाकर का ध्यान करते हैं। हम सप्ताश्व (सात घोड़ों वाले) को जानते हैं, सहस्रकिरण का ध्यान करते हैं, वह रवि हमें प्रेरित करें।
benefits: Three powerful Gayatri mantras dedicated to Lord Surya
Enhances vitality, energy, and radiance when chanted at sunrise
Traditionally recited for removal of eye diseases and skin ailments
Strengthens the Sun in one's horoscope
Ideal for Surya Namaskar practitioners and Sunday worship
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Sun God Surya, Surya Gayatri (सूर्य गायत्री) stands as a powerful Vedic meditation mantra. Attributed to Rig Veda, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove eye diseases and skin ailments as well as strengthen the Sun in one's horoscope. Additionally, it is known to enhance vitality, energy, and radiance when chanted at sunrise. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti, Ratha Saptami, and Chhath Puja for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 3 verses and takes around 2 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Surya Gayatri in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Surya Gayatri mantra and what does it mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Gayatri (सूर्य गायत्री) is a 24-syllable Vedic meditation mantra addressed to Surya, the Sun God, structured in the classical Gayatri chhanda (meter) like the original Savitri-Gayatri of Rishi Vishvamitra. The most common form runs: Om Bhaskaraya Vidmahe Mahadyutikaraya Dhimahi, Tannah Adityah Prachodayat — 'Om, may we know Bhaskara (the Maker of Light); may we meditate on the Great Luminary; may that Aditya inspire and illuminate our intellect.' Each deity-Gayatri replaces Savitur with the chosen devata's name, and the Surya/Aditya Gayatri is among the most chanted such forms because Surya is the visible Brahman who sustains all life. The mantra is preserved in classical Vedic upasana tradition. (Source: Vedic Surya upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Surya Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Surya Gayatri is traditionally chanted to (1) strengthen a weak or afflicted Sun (Surya graha) in the birth chart — Sun rules confidence, vitality, leadership, the soul (atman karaka), and the father; (2) cure or protect against eye diseases (since Sun rules the right eye for males and left for females), skin ailments, low Vitamin D, and bone-related issues; (3) enhance ojas, tejas and overall vitality through pranayama-style sun-breathing; (4) grant career advancement, government favour, and recognition; and (5) deepen spiritual sadhana by linking the chanter to the visible Pratyaksha Brahman of the Vedas. Sun astrologers (jyotishis) often prescribe 108 daily chants on Sundays for 40 days as a Surya remedy. (Sources: Vedic jyotisha tradition; classical Surya upasana texts.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Surya Gayatri be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Gayatri is most powerfully chanted at sunrise (Brahma Muhurta or as the sun's disc appears on the horizon) facing east, ideally while offering Arghya (water libation) from a copper kalasha — the classical Surya Arghya practice. Special days include every Sunday (Ravivar — Surya's day), Ratha Saptami (the Sun's birthday in Magha Shukla Saptami), Makara Sankranti (Sun's entry into Capricorn), Chhath Puja (especially in Bihar-UP), every Sankranti (monthly Sun-transit), and Sundays falling in Hasta, Uttara-Phalguni or Uttara-Ashadha nakshatra (Sun's nakshatras). Recommended count is 108 (one mala) daily, increased to 1008 for serious Surya sadhana, combined with Surya Namaskar yoga and a vegetarian sattvic diet. (Sources: Surya upasana paddhati; Ratha Saptami vrata vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Surya Gayatri different from the original Vedic Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Gayatri-meter mantras but with key differences. (1) The original Gayatri Mantra (Rigveda 3.62.10 by Rishi Vishvamitra) — Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat — addresses Savitur, the universal solar-creative principle, and is the supreme mantra of all Vedas, recited by every initiated Brahmin in Sandhyavandana. (2) The Surya Gayatri is a deity-specific Gayatri (devata-Gayatri) that swaps in Bhaskara/Aditya for Savitur, making it specifically focused on Surya as a worshipable form rather than the abstract solar principle. There exist parallel deity-Gayatris for Ganesha, Shiva, Vishnu, Durga, Lakshmi, Hanuman, Narasimha, etc. — all built on the same 24-syllable Gayatri framework. (Sources: Rigveda 3.62.10; deity-Gayatri tradition in Devata Upasana texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Surya Gayatri be chanted by women and non-Brahmins?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — while the original Vedic Gayatri Mantra has traditional restrictions (recited only by upanayana-initiated dvija males in strict orthodox Smarta tradition), the deity-Gayatris like the Surya Gayatri are open to all sincere devotees regardless of gender, caste, or sampradaya. Most modern acharyas including Swami Vivekananda, Sri Ramakrishna tradition, the Arya Samaj, and ISKCON explicitly affirm that women and all devotees can chant Gayatri-based mantras. The Surya Gayatri is particularly popular in households where there is illness involving the Sun-ruled body parts (eyes, heart, bones) or where the family astrologer has prescribed Surya remediation. Chhath Puja in Bihar — performed primarily by women — is essentially a community-scale Surya upasana. (Sources: Modern acharya consensus; Chhath Puja tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What other Sun mantras and stotras complement the Surya Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Gayatri is most commonly recited alongside (1) Aditya Hridayam (Valmiki Ramayana Yuddha Kanda 105) — taught by Rishi Agastya to Rama before slaying Ravana, the foremost Sun-stotra; (2) Surya Ashtakam — the 8-verse Sun hymn from Samba Purana; (3) Surya Sahasranama from the Skanda Purana; (4) the 12 names of Surya — Mitra, Ravi, Surya, Bhanu, Khaga, Pushan, Hiranyagarbha, Marichi, Aditya, Savitr, Arka, Bhaskara — chanted with each Surya Namaskar; (5) Suryashtakam by Sri Sambhu and (6) Navagraha Surya Beeja Mantra Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namaha. A complete morning Surya sadhana typically combines Surya Namaskar, Surya Arghya, Surya Gayatri (108×), Aditya Hridayam (1×), and the 12 Sun-names. (Sources: Aditya Hridayam; Samba Purana; classical Surya upasana paddhati.)
